title: Tutcheria sophiae
intro: Tutcheria sophiae, also known as Sophia's Tutcheria or Sophia's tree, is a plant species native to Sri Lanka. It has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and respiratory issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Tutcheria sophiae include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Tutcheria sophiae is commonly used to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and decoctions, which are used to treat respiratory infections, coughs, and fevers.
taxonomy: Tutcheria sophiae is a genus of flowering plants in the family Rubiaceae. It is native to tropical Africa and was first discovered by the British botanist John Hutchinson. The classification of Tutcheria sophiae is further divided into the species T. sophiae, which is the only recognized species within the genus. Tutcheria sophiae is part of the order Gentianales and is closely related to other Rubiaceae species. The plant's classification is based on its morphological characteristics and genetic relationships with other plants in the same family.
